_doc: Raven Hollow hidden zones / easter-eggs. Consumed by SecretSystem (scripts/systems/secret_system.gd). A lore-accurate, tasteful twist on Diablo 2's Secret Cow Level: instead of cubing Wirt's Leg + a Tome of Town Portal, the player COMBINES specific relics at a specific SHRINE to tear a 'discrepancy' in the Bloodstone's ledger and open a hidden pocket zone. Each secret: shrine (where the ritual is performed), ritual.items (relic ids the ritual consumes/checks), reward_table (a LootSystem table id, rolled on success), reward_fallback (used when LootSystem is absent), discovery (toast text), lore. Shrines are described in 'shrines'.

cairn_of_the_unfiled.name: Cairn of the Unfiled
cairn_of_the_unfiled.flavor: A grave-cairn at the edge of the red fields, where the childrens' shoes are laid. The Bloodstone never got its name.

cut_thread_altar.name: Altar of the Cut Thread
cut_thread_altar.flavor: A transmission stone with a single filament still humming, severed but not silent.

detectives_marker.name: The Collector's Chalk Mark
detectives_marker.flavor: A discrepancy chalked on the chamber wall where Kriggar first woke and noticed the world was wrong.

unfiled_field.name: The Unfiled Field

discovery.title: A Discrepancy Opens
discovery.line: The cairn cannot reconcile a name against a ledger that erased it. A seam tears in the world's record.
discovery.subtitle: Hidden: The Unfiled Field

unfiled_field.lore: The famine erased a generation before it buried the queen. Press a relic of one the Bloodstone unwrote against a page of its own debt-ledger, and the index chokes on the contradiction. What spills out is a furrowed fold where the Unfiled still stand at harvest -- mute grave-shells the record could not file, and the First Discrepancy that walks their rows. Farmable. Do not mistake it for mercy.

threadbare_fold.name: The Threadbare Fold

discovery.title: The Thread Answers
discovery.line: A severed filament, rung once with a grave-bell, remembers the shell it used to hold.
discovery.subtitle: Hidden: The Threadbare Fold

threadbare_fold.lore: Cut a thread and the shell drops -- but the thread does not forget its knot. Ring a grave-bell over a filament Lilith let go, and the empty length folds back on itself into a pocket that never finished dying: the Threadbare Fold, where half-unwound shells drift between wills.

collectors_office.name: The Collector's Office

discovery.title: Case Reopened
discovery.line: You lay a found mark under a cracked loupe on the chalk. The discrepancy notices you back.
discovery.subtitle: Hidden: The Collector's Office

collectors_office.lore: Kriggar solved the world by noticing what did not add up. Set a lens on a mark he would have filed, and his office assembles itself out of the noticing: a small dry room of pinned threads and dead men's names, where the case is always still open. An easter-egg homage; the loupe and the mark are returned to you.
